meta.description: We build complete AI-powered WooCommerce stores with server-side tracking, headless Nuxt frontend, and AI content pipeline. Catch every conversion signal.

problem.label: The problem
problem.subtitle: Three invisible leaks drain your ad budget every day. Most WooCommerce stores have all three.
problem.savingsDesc: Estimated monthly loss for a store spending €10k/month on ads — from tracking gaps alone.
problem.card0_desc: iPhone purchases vanish before reaching Meta ML — your campaigns optimise on a fraction of actual buyers.
problem.card1_desc: Over a third of Polish internet users run adblockers. Every one of them is invisible to Google Ads' Smart Bidding.
problem.card2_desc: Typical WooCommerce scores 40–60 on mobile. You pay 20–40% more per click than you should.

services.label: What we build
services.title: Three layers. One complete store.
services.subtitle: StoreKite wraps your WooCommerce with infrastructure that makes your store faster, smarter, and trackable.

tracking.name: Tracking

tracking.description: Server-side tagging runs on our infrastructure. iOS, adblockers, GDPR — none of them touch your conversion data.

timeline.title: From zero to live in 4–8 weeks.

faq.label: FAQ
faq.title: Questions we hear often.
faq.item0_a: We build complete AI-powered WooCommerce stores — headless Nuxt 3 frontend, automated GA4/GTM tracking, AI content pipeline (blog, social, email), and MCP-powered WordPress management. It's a full store build, not just tracking consulting.
faq.item1_q: How is this different from a regular agency?
faq.item1_a: Regular agencies take 3–6 months and skip tracking infrastructure. We deliver in 4–8 weeks with enterprise-grade server-side tracking, AI content automation, and MCP-powered WordPress management built in from day one.
faq.item2_q: Do I need to migrate from my current store?
faq.item2_a: Yes — we build a new headless frontend on Nuxt 3 while keeping your WooCommerce backend. Your products, orders, and customers stay in WordPress. The frontend gets a complete rebuild for performance and tracking.
faq.item3_a: We handle 301 redirects, canonical URLs, structured data, and sitemap generation. Most stores see improved rankings within weeks thanks to Core Web Vitals improvements.
faq.item4_q: How many projects do you take per year?
faq.item4_a: 5–8 projects maximum. Each store gets dedicated attention — no assembly line, no junior devs. When we're full, we're full.
faq.item5_q: What does the AI content pipeline actually do?
faq.item5_a: It generates SEO blog articles, social media drafts, and email campaigns based on your products and brand voice. You review and approve before publishing. It's not auto-posting garbage — it's AI-assisted content creation with human oversight.
faq.item6_a: Pricing depends on the number of products, required integrations, and project complexity. Every store is different — we provide a detailed quote after a free consultation call. Book a call and we'll give you a transparent estimate within 48 hours.
faq.item7_q: Do I need an existing online store?
faq.item7_a: No — we can build from scratch or rebuild your existing store. If you already have a WooCommerce shop, we keep your products, orders, and customers intact while replacing the frontend. Starting fresh? We handle everything from setup to launch.
faq.item8_q: What integrations do you support beyond the standard stack?
faq.item8_a: Beyond GA4, GTM, and Meta CAPI we integrate with payment gateways (PayU, Stripe, PayPo), shipping providers (InPost, DHL, DPD), marketing platforms (Klaviyo, Mailchimp), marketplaces (Allegro, Amazon via BaseLinker), ERP systems, and more. If it has an API, we can connect it.
faq.item9_q: Does it have to be WooCommerce?
faq.item9_a: We specialize in WooCommerce + headless Nuxt — that's where we deliver the fastest and best results. Migration from other platforms (Shopify, PrestaShop, Magento) is possible, but timelines and costs may be higher depending on complexity. Book a call and we'll assess your situation honestly.

cta.title: Ready to build your AI store?
cta.subtitle: 30-minute call. We'll map out your store build — tech stack, AI content pipeline, tracking setup, timeline and price.
cta.fine: No commitment · You keep everything we build

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: At StoreKite, we take your privacy seriously. This Privacy Policy explains what personal data we collect, how we use it, and what rights you have regarding your information. We are committed to handling your data in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and applicable Polish law.
privacy.s1_body: The data controller is Szymon Bubała, operating StoreKite — an AI-powered e-commerce development service. Registered in Poland. Contact for privacy matters: hello@storekite.ai. Websites: storekite.ai and storekite.com.
privacy.s2_p1: We collect personal data in the following situations:
privacy.s2_li0: Name, email address, company name, and any other information you submit via our contact form
privacy.s2_li1: Name, email address, company details, and scheduling preferences when you book a discovery call via Cal.com
privacy.s2_li2: Any information you voluntarily share in email correspondence or during calls
privacy.s2_auto_li0: IP address, browser type, operating system, and device type
privacy.s2_auto_li1: Pages visited, time on page, scroll depth, and click patterns
privacy.s2_auto_li2: Traffic source, search queries, and referral URLs
privacy.s2_auto_note: Automatically collected data is gathered through Google Analytics 4 and Vercel Analytics. See Section 5 for details on how these tools handle your data.
privacy.s3_intro: We process your personal data on the following legal bases under Article 6 of the GDPR:
privacy.s3_li0: Consent (Art. 6(1)(a)): analytics cookies and any optional marketing communications
privacy.s3_li1: Contract performance (Art. 6(1)(b)): processing necessary to deliver services you have engaged us for
privacy.s3_li2: Legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f)): website security, fraud prevention, and improving our services based on usage patterns
privacy.s3_li3: Legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c)): accounting records, invoicing, and tax compliance required by Polish law
privacy.s4_intro: We use your personal data to:
privacy.s4_li0: Respond to your inquiries, messages, and contact form submissions
privacy.s4_li1: Schedule and conduct discovery calls and project consultations
privacy.s4_li2: Deliver and manage our e-commerce development services
privacy.s4_li3: Issue invoices and maintain required accounting records
privacy.s4_li4: Analyze website traffic and improve our website and services
privacy.s5_intro: Our website uses cookies and similar tracking technologies. Cookies are small text files stored on your device that help us provide a better experience.
privacy.s5_essential_body: Essential cookies are necessary for the website to function correctly. They enable core features such as session management and security. These cookies cannot be disabled and do not require consent.
privacy.s5_ga_body: We use Google Analytics 4 with IP anonymization enabled to understand how visitors interact with our website. GA4 uses cookies to collect anonymized usage data including pages visited, session duration, and conversion events. Data is processed by Google LLC (USA) under Standard Contractual Clauses. Analytics data is retained for 14 months. You can opt out using the Google Analytics Opt-out Browser Add-on (tools.google.com/dlpage/gaoptout).
privacy.s5_vercel_body: We use Vercel Analytics for website performance monitoring. Unlike traditional analytics, Vercel Analytics does not use cookies and does not store personally identifiable information. It measures Core Web Vitals and page load metrics in an aggregated, privacy-preserving manner. Data is processed by Vercel Inc. (USA).
privacy.s5_manage: You can manage cookie preferences at any time through your browser settings. Blocking essential cookies may affect website functionality.
privacy.s6_intro: We work with the following third-party services that may process your personal data on our behalf:
privacy.s6_calcom_body: When you book a discovery call through our website, you interact with Cal.com's scheduling platform. Cal.com processes your name, email address, and scheduling preferences to book and manage your appointment. Cal.com is GDPR-compliant. For more information, see Cal.com's Privacy Policy at cal.com/privacy.
privacy.s6_google_body: Analytics data may be transferred to Google LLC servers in the United States. This transfer is governed by Standard Contractual Clauses as approved by the European Commission. For more information, see Google's Privacy Policy at policies.google.com/privacy.
privacy.s6_vercel_body: Our website is hosted on Vercel's infrastructure. Vercel may process server logs and technical request data as part of providing hosting services. For more information, see Vercel's Privacy Policy at vercel.com/legal/privacy-policy.
privacy.s7_intro: We retain personal data only for as long as necessary for the purpose for which it was collected:
privacy.s7_li0: Contact form and inquiry data: 3 years from last contact
privacy.s7_li1: Service agreement and project data: 5 years (required by Polish accounting and tax law)
privacy.s7_li2: Analytics data: 14 months (as configured in Google Analytics 4)
privacy.s7_li3: You may request deletion of your data at any time (subject to legally required retention periods)
privacy.s8_intro: As a data subject under the GDPR, you have the following rights:
privacy.s8_li0: Right of access (Art. 15): obtain confirmation and a copy of your personal data we process
privacy.s8_li1: Right to rectification (Art. 16): request correction of inaccurate or incomplete data
privacy.s8_li2: Right to erasure (Art. 17): request deletion of your data ("right to be forgotten")
privacy.s8_li3: Right to restrict processing (Art. 18): request that we limit how we use your data
privacy.s8_li4: Right to data portability (Art. 20): receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format
privacy.s8_li5: Right to object (Art. 21): object to processing based on legitimate interest
privacy.s8_li6: Right to withdraw consent (Art. 7(3)): withdraw any consent given at any time without affecting previous processing
privacy.s8_contact: To exercise any of these rights, contact us at hello@storekite.ai. We will respond within 30 days.
privacy.s8_uodo: You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the supervisory authority. In Poland: Urząd Ochrony Danych Osobowych (UODO), ul. Stawki 2, 00-193 Warszawa, uodo.gov.pl.
privacy.s9_body: Some of the third-party services we use (Google Analytics, Vercel) are operated by companies based in the United States. When data is transferred outside the European Economic Area (EEA), we ensure appropriate safeguards are in place, including Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) as approved by the European Commission, or reliance on an adequacy decision such as the EU-US Data Privacy Framework.
privacy.s10_body: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices or applicable law. The date of the most recent update is displayed at the top of this page. We encourage you to review this policy periodically. Continued use of our website following any changes constitutes your acceptance of the updated policy.
privacy.s11_body: For any questions, concerns, or requests related to this Privacy Policy or the processing of your personal data, please contact:

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These Terms of Service ("Terms") govern the relationship between StoreKite (operated by Szymon Bubała, Poland) and any client who engages StoreKite's services. By booking a discovery call, signing a project agreement, or otherwise engaging our services, you agree to these Terms.
terms.s1_p1: StoreKite is a trading name operated by Szymon Bubała, an entrepreneur registered in Poland under Polish law (NIP/tax registration details provided upon request). Registered office: Poland. Contact: hello@storekite.ai.
terms.s1_p2: These Terms apply to all services provided by StoreKite unless a separate written agreement explicitly supersedes specific provisions.
terms.s2_intro: StoreKite provides AI-powered e-commerce development services, including but not limited to:
terms.s2_li1: Server-side tracking setup (Google Analytics 4, Google Tag Manager, Meta Conversions API)
terms.s2_li2: AI content pipeline configuration (automated blog, social media drafts, email automation)
terms.s2_li5: Optional ongoing AI Care retainer (content management, monitoring, maintenance)
terms.s2_note: The exact scope, deliverables, timeline, and pricing for each engagement are defined in a separate Project Agreement signed by both parties before work begins.
terms.s3_discovery_body: All projects begin with a free 30-minute discovery call to assess your needs, review your current technical setup, and determine project fit. There is no obligation to proceed.
terms.s3_agreement_body: Before any billable work begins, StoreKite prepares a detailed Project Agreement specifying scope, deliverables, timeline, and price. Work commences only after the Project Agreement is countersigned by both parties and the initial payment has been received.
terms.s3_communication_body: Primary communication takes place via email. StoreKite aims to respond to client communications within 1 business day. Project progress is shared via agreed tools or regular status updates as specified in the Project Agreement.
terms.s3_revisions_body: Each project includes a defined number of revision rounds as specified in the Project Agreement. Revisions within scope are included. Change requests that expand the agreed project scope will be assessed and quoted separately at StoreKite's standard rate.
terms.s4_pricing_body: All prices are quoted and invoiced in EUR. VAT may be applicable depending on the client's country and business registration status. Polish VAT rules apply to clients based in Poland.
terms.s4_li0: Initial payment: 50% of the agreed project value, due before work begins
terms.s4_li1: Final payment: 50% of the agreed project value, due before final delivery and deployment
terms.s4_li2: AI Care retainer services: billed monthly in advance via bank transfer or card
terms.s4_terms_body: Invoices are payable within 14 days of issuance. Late payments accrue statutory interest as provided under Polish law (Ustawa o terminach zapłaty w transakcjach handlowych). StoreKite reserves the right to suspend work on outstanding projects until overdue invoices are settled.
terms.s4_cancel_intro: If the client cancels the project after work has commenced:
terms.s4_cancel_li0: Before 25% completion: the initial payment (50% of project value) is non-refundable
terms.s4_cancel_li1: After 25% completion: all payments made to date are non-refundable
terms.s4_cancel_li2: All completed deliverables produced up to the point of cancellation remain the client's property upon receipt of all due payments
terms.s5_yours_body: Upon receipt of full payment, all custom code, configurations, designs, and content created specifically for the client's project become the client's property. StoreKite transfers all applicable intellectual property rights to the client.
terms.s5_ours_body: StoreKite retains ownership of its proprietary tools, internal frameworks, templates, methodologies, and pre-existing code used in building the client's project. A non-exclusive, irrevocable license to use these components within the delivered project is granted to the client.
terms.s5_portfolio_body: Unless explicitly requested otherwise in writing before project commencement, StoreKite retains the right to reference the project in its portfolio and to use anonymized, aggregated data (without disclosing confidential business information or trade secrets) for marketing and case study purposes.
terms.s6_p1: StoreKite provides services as described and agreed in the Project Agreement. We make no warranties beyond what is explicitly stated in the Project Agreement.
terms.s6_intro: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law:
terms.s6_li0: StoreKite is not liable for indirect, incidental, consequential, special, or punitive damages, including loss of revenue or profit
terms.s6_li1: StoreKite's total liability for any claim is limited to the total amount paid by the client for the specific service that gave rise to the claim
terms.s6_li2: StoreKite is not liable for service interruptions, data loss, or errors caused by third-party services (hosting providers, payment gateways, analytics platforms, etc.)
terms.s7_li0: 90 days of bug-fix support for defects in StoreKite's deliverables (Growth Store and AI Store packages)
terms.s7_li1: Bug-fix support covers defects arising from StoreKite's own work; it does not cover issues caused by third-party changes, client modifications, or scope changes
terms.s7_after: After the 90-day support period, ongoing support is available through the AI Care monthly retainer or on a time-and-materials basis at StoreKite's standard hourly rate.
terms.s8_intro: If you are dissatisfied with StoreKite's services, please follow this process:
terms.s8_li0: Contact us at hello@storekite.ai with a written description of the issue
terms.s8_li1: StoreKite will acknowledge your complaint within 2 business days
terms.s8_li2: StoreKite will work in good faith to investigate and resolve the issue within 14 days
terms.s8_li3: If agreement cannot be reached, disputes are subject to the governing law and jurisdiction specified in Section 9
terms.s9_p1: These Terms are governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Poland. Any disputes arising from or relating to these Terms or any Project Agreement shall be resolved by the competent civil courts having jurisdiction over StoreKite's place of business in Poland.
terms.s9_b2c: For consumers (B2C): Nothing in these Terms limits or excludes any rights you may have under applicable consumer protection legislation.
terms.s9_b2b: For businesses (B2B): Disputes shall be resolved exclusively by the court competent for StoreKite's registered place of business.
terms.s10_body: StoreKite may update these Terms of Service from time to time. The date of the most recent revision is shown at the top of this page. For ongoing projects, the Terms in effect at the time of signing the Project Agreement apply for the duration of that project.
terms.s11_body: For questions or concerns regarding these Terms of Service, please contact:
